mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of instances where GDPC1 shows consecutive quarterly acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity valuations, leading to negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.05 when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong labor market conditions (rising PAYEMS combined with falling UNRATE) signal robust consumer demand, supporting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX declines in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where industrial production exceeds 103, supply-side momentum translates quickly into earnings upgrades, boosting S&P 500 over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX drops in > 60% of instances where INDPRO breaks above 103 during peak expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflationary pressure (rising CPIAUCSL) forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX rises consistently over 60-day windows despite accelerating CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.4 million units signals weakening demand and economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX remains positive when HOUST falls below 1400 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) coinciding with expanding real GDP (GDPC1) confirms broad-based economic strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX declines when both RSAFS and GDPC1 show concurrent positive growth over a 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market regime where unemployment stays below 4.0%, wage growth supports consumer spending, driving short-term positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX falls when UNRATE remains below 4.0% for extended periods without corresponding earnings growth.
